title: In the Desert
author: J.C. Greenburg
desc: Ant-sized Andrew, Judy & Thudd are blown across the Australian desert and must survive dust devils, deadly snakes, and a whistling spider to reach Uluru!

q.en: At the start of the story, where are ant-sized Andrew and Judy riding through the desert?
q.zh: 故事一开始，变成蚂蚁那么小的安德鲁和朱迪是坐在什么地方穿过沙漠的？

options.en: On the back of a kangaroo
In a bottle cap glued to Uncle Al's jeep dashboard
options.zh: 坐在粘在阿尔叔叔吉普车仪表盘上的一个瓶盖里
在 Goa 巨蛇机器里面

options.en: In a nest of the whistling spider

explain.en: Uncle Al glued an empty bottle cap to the jeep's dashboard to make a safe perch where Andrew and Judy could watch the Australian desert.
explain.zh: 阿尔叔叔把一个空瓶盖粘在吉普车的仪表盘上，做成一个小平台，让安德鲁和朱迪可以安全地观看澳大利亚沙漠。

q.en: What do Australians call the spinning column of hot air and sand that blows Andrew and Judy away?
q.zh: 澳大利亚人把那股把安德鲁和朱迪吹走的、旋转着的热空气和沙子的柱子叫做什么？

explain.en: Thudd explains it is called a dust devil, and 'Australian people call it willy-willy.' Super-hot air near the sand spins and picks up sand.
explain.zh: 撒得解释说它叫尘暴(dust devil)，“澳大利亚人叫它威利-威利(willy-willy)”。靠近沙子的超热空气旋转起来，卷起了沙子。

q.en: How long can a water-holding frog stay underground inside its skin cocoon?
q.zh: 蓄水蛙(frog)待在地下的皮肤茧里能坚持多久？

explain.en: Thudd says the water-holding frog drinks lots of water when it rains, digs a tunnel, makes a cocoon from old skin, and 'can stay underground for five years!'
explain.zh: 撒得说蓄水蛙下雨时喝很多水，挖一条地道，用旧皮做成茧，能“在地下待上五年！”

q.en: What makes the katydid and the meat ants finally let go of Andrew and Judy?
q.zh: 是什么让螽斯(katydid)和食肉蚁(meat ants)终于放开了安德鲁和朱迪？

options.zh: 安德鲁巨大的大蒜味打嗝

explain.en: Thudd says katydids and crickets do not like garlic and smell with their knees. Andrew's big garlic burp makes them release their grip.
explain.zh: 撒得说螽斯和蟋蟀不喜欢大蒜，而且它们是用膝盖闻味的。安德鲁的大蒜味打嗝让它们松开了爪子。

q.en: In the Australian desert, what is a 'mob'?
q.zh: 在澳大利亚沙漠里，“mob”指的是什么？

explain.en: When dozens of leaping kangaroos appear, Thudd says, 'Bunch of kangaroos called mob.'
explain.zh: 当成群跳跃的袋鼠出现时，撒得说：“一群袋鼠叫做 mob。”

q.en: Which small marsupial attacks the scorpion and saves Judy from the spider and scorpion?
q.zh: 是哪只小型有袋动物攻击了蝎子，把朱迪从蜘蛛和蝎子手中救了出来？

explain.en: A fat-tailed dunnart leaps onto the scorpion and turns it on its back. Thudd says it is a marsupial like a kangaroo, with tiny babies that crawl into its pouch.
explain.zh: 一只肥尾狭足袋鼩跳到蝎子身上把它掀翻。撒得说它和袋鼠一样是有袋动物，小宝宝小得像一粒米，会爬进育儿袋里。

q.en: According to True Stuff, how does a gecko keep its eyes clean?
q.zh: 根据“真实知识”(True Stuff)，壁虎是怎么清洁眼睛的？

options.en: It licks them with its tongue

explain.en: True Stuff says geckos cannot blink because they have no eyelids. A transparent scale covers their eyes, and they clean the scale with their tongues.
explain.zh: “真实知识”说壁虎没有眼皮，不会眨眼睛。一层透明的鳞片盖着眼睛，它们用舌头来舔干净这层鳞片。

q.en: What does Andrew use to hook a rolling tumbleweed and ride it toward Uluru?
q.zh: 安德鲁用什么钩住滚动的风滚草(tumbleweed)，骑着它前往乌卢鲁(Uluru)？

explain.en: Andrew wraps the Drastic Elastic around himself and Judy and tosses his flashlight (hooked to it) at a tumbleweed. It snags the weed 'like a big fish.'
explain.zh: 安德鲁把弹力橡皮筋缠在自己和朱迪身上，把连着它的手电筒朝风滚草扔去。手电筒像“钓到大鱼”一样钩住了草团。

q.zh: 在“真实知识”里，“夏眠”(estivation) 是什么意思？

options.en: Slowing the body down to survive heat and dryness
options.zh: 让身体慢下来，以熬过炎热和干旱

explain.en: True Stuff explains that when it gets too hot and dry, desert frogs burrow underground and slow their bodies down. That is called estivation.
explain.zh: “真实知识”解释说，天气太热太干时，沙漠蛙会钻到地下，让身体慢下来。这叫做夏眠(estivation)。

q.en: What is Uluru, the place where Uncle Al and Winka wait for the kids?
q.zh: 乌卢鲁(Uluru)是什么地方？阿尔叔叔和温卡在那儿等孩子们。

options.en: The highest rock in the desert, with hotels and restaurants
options.zh: 沙漠里最高的岩石，那里有旅馆和餐馆

explain.en: Uncle Al tells the kids Uluru is 'the highest spot in this desert' and such a beautiful place that people come to see it, with hotels and restaurants.
explain.zh: 阿尔叔叔告诉孩子们，乌卢鲁是“这片沙漠里最高的地方”，而且非常美丽，人们都来参观，那里还有旅馆和餐馆。
